title: Trade and International Business: Research & Analysis | CSIS
gist: CSIS's trade hub centers on the U.S. decision not to renew USMCA (triggering a decade of annual reviews and investment uncertainty), Mexico's 14-to-1 China import imbalance, Alcoa's landmark South Africa aluminum bet, and South Korea's pivot to becoming an AI-age 'ecosystem power.'

insights.claim: The United States declined to renew USMCA for another 16 years, triggering a decade of annual reviews; the deal did not collapse but now faces prolonged uncertainty that stalls investment across North America.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Annual-review limbo raises the discount rate on North American cross-border capex; watch delayed FDI decisions.
insights.locator: The United States Has Opted Not to Extend the USMCA

insights.claim: Bill Reinsch argues the USMCA review will prolong uncertainty, delaying investment and complicating North American trade negotiations rather than resolving them.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Confirms base case that the review process itself, not any single outcome, is the drag on regional investment.

insights.claim: Mexico is the hinge of North American economic security as the top U.S. trading partner, yet imports from China at a 14-to-1 ratio, exposing a backdoor for Chinese goods into the USMCA bloc.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: The 14:1 import imbalance is the leverage point for rules-of-origin tightening and transshipment scrutiny.

insights.claim: Alcoa has made the largest U.S. mining supply chain investment in South Africa in years, a bet whose payoff depends on South Africa's policy choices and its ability to compete for strategic mining capital.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: A test case for whether Western capital returns to African mining; SA mining policy is the swing variable.

insights.claim: A CSIS brief maps trade value and goods types across eight distinct South China Sea chokepoints, challenging assumptions about which chokepoints matter most and each country's relative reliance.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Granular chokepoint dependency data reprices which maritime disruptions actually threaten specific economies.

insights.claim: Navin Girishankar argues South Korea's second 'Miracle on the Han River' hinges on becoming an ecosystem power that fuses capital, standards, technology, and know-how, rather than remaining a pure export nation, for the AI industrial age.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Reframes Korea's tech competitiveness from export volume to standards-and-ecosystem control; relevant to Samsung/SK Hynix strategy.

insights.claim: The U.S. is pursuing Section 301 investigations into whether Chinese forced labor and overcapacity are harming U.S. trade interests, keeping a tariff-escalation channel open.
insights.speaker: CSIS
insights.so_what: Section 301 findings could seed new tariffs on overcapacity sectors; a forward indicator of trade-war reescalation.
